subject: Databases
title: Outbox Pattern
description: A short lesson to help you apply Outbox Pattern.

hook.text: Save an order and send an email. The order saves but the email fails. Or the email sends but the order doesn't save.

buildup.text: You can't put a database write and an external call in the same transaction. They're different systems.

discovery.text: The outbox pattern: save the order AND an outbox message in the same transaction. A worker processes the outbox later.

twist.text: The worker reads the outbox, sends the email, and marks the message as processed. If it fails, it retries.

climax.text: The outbox guarantees at-least-once delivery. Combined with idempotency, you get exactly-once effect.

punchline.text: Outbox: write the intent to the database. Process it asynchronously.

quiz.question: Why can't you combine a database write and an external API call in one transaction?
options: They're separate systems — the database can't roll back an email or API call
Databases don't support any external communication
API calls are always faster than database writes
